topic: A person finds out their surprise party has been cancelled
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with the dull thud of something unexpected. My fingers hovered over the screen for a full minute, a slow, deliberate movement. I read the subject line—"Party Update"—and then the body of the message. A collective intake of breath escaped me, followed by a low, rumbling chuckle that started deep in my chest and vibrated through my ribs. I reread the sentence three times, just to be absolutely certain. The absurdity of it all—all the planning, the decorations, the carefully chosen theme—crumbling into this single, polite notification. A wide grin stretched across my face, pulling at the corners of my eyes. I leaned back in my chair, letting out a soft, breathy laugh that sounded entirely too loud in the quiet apartment. "Well," I murmured to the empty room, tapping a rhythm on the desk, "that certainly changes things."

[story 2]

I picked up my phone, intending to text Sarah, but stopped. The message on the screen—the official cancellation notice for my milestone birthday bash—just seemed to mock me. A strange lightness flooded my limbs, making my shoulders relax in a way they hadn't in weeks. I let out a short, sharp burst of air that morphed into a full, unrestrained laugh. It wasn't a polite chuckle; it was a genuine, bubbling sound that made my stomach clench pleasantly. I paced the length of my living room, a slight, springy energy propelling me forward. I stopped by the window, looking out at the street, and shook my head slowly, a silent, joyful acknowledgment of the ridiculousness of the situation. "Of course," I whispered, a soft, delighted sound, "the universe has a sense of humor."

[story 3]

When the confirmation arrived, I didn't immediately process the information. Instead, I found myself leaning against the kitchen counter, my head tilted back as I let out a sound that started as a soft snort and built into a full, unrestrained peal of laughter. My hands gripped the edge of the counter, knuckles turning white, but it wasn't tension; it was pure, unadulterated glee. I wiped a stray tear from my eye, the moisture mixing with the sheer mirth bubbling up inside me. I looked around my suddenly empty space, a wide, almost silly smile playing on my lips. "This is rich," I managed to say, the words punctuated by another burst of delighted snorts. I started tapping my foot rapidly against the tile floor, a restless, happy energy radiating from me.
